genre.title: Genre
genre.description: Musical genre classification, era influences, and cultural roots

genre.title: Genre
genre.description: Detailed genres from primary to least, ordered by greatest influence
genre.x-instruction: Start with a general genre, but then list genre specifics that makes this song different within its own genre
genre.x-example: Bubblegum Pop, Trap Metal, East Coast Boom-Bap, Street Rap

era_influence.title: Era Influence
era_influence.description: Decade or period the sound draws from
era_influence.x-instruction: What era of music is this being influenced by? Start with the year, but list the specific style from that time period that influenced
era_influence.x-example: late 80s synth-pop, 2010s SoundCloud rap, 70s Philly soul

cultural_origin.title: Cultural Origin
cultural_origin.description: Regional or cultural roots and specific stylistic niche
cultural_origin.x-instruction: What culture and the style within the culture is this song being influenced by?
cultural_origin.x-example: Korean idol pop, Lagos Afrobeat, European Italo-Disco, Caribbean Dancehall, Polska Polka

vocals.title: Vocals
vocals.description: Vocal performance, cast, timbre, style, effects, and backing arrangement

cast.title: Vocal Cast
cast.description: Each distinct vocalist on the track and their characteristics
cast.x-instruction: Identify all distinct vocalists on the track. Create one entry per voice, describing their role, tone, and style.

role.title: Role
role.description: What sections this voice performs and their part
role.x-instruction: Note their gender/tone and assign which sections of the song they perform.
role.x-example: solo male rapper on verses, female soul singer on hook/chorus; lead on all sections

timbre.title: Timbre
timbre.description: Physical texture and tonal quality of this voice
timbre.x-instruction: Describe the unique sonic character of this vocalist's voice. Use physical and textural adjectives.
timbre.x-example: warm and husky, raspy soul tone with raw grit, bright and nasal, deep resonant baritone

range.title: Range
range.x-instruction: Identify how high or low this vocalist sings, and note what vocal register (chest, head, falsetto) they utilize.
range.x-example: alto chest voice, tenor with falsetto in chorus, spans 3 octaves from low growl to whistle register

style.title: Style
style.x-instruction: Describe how this vocalist delivers the performance. Is it sung, rapped, spoken, or something else?
style.x-example: belting, whisper-singing, rapid-fire rap, spoken-word, yodeling between registers, melodic sing-rap

sync.title: Sync
sync.description: How this voice rides and locks with the beat
sync.x-instruction: Analyze the rhythmic timing of this vocalist relative to the instrumental track.
sync.x-example: tight rhythmic phrasing locked to grid, lazy dragging behind the beat, staccato machine-gun syllables

emotion.title: Emotion
emotion.description: Emotional intensity and character of delivery
emotion.x-instruction: Determine the feeling or attitude this vocalist is projecting through their performance.
emotion.x-example: vulnerable and pleading, aggressive and confrontational, detached and deadpan, joyful and carefree

accent.title: Accent
accent.x-instruction: Listen for any specific regional accents or cultural dialects present in the pronunciation of the words.
accent.x-example: Southern US drawl, heavy New York street inflection, Caribbean Patois flavor, neutral/no notable accent

languages.title: Languages
languages.x-instruction: List all languages this vocalist sings in, even if only a single phrase.
languages.x-example: English, Spanish (bridge only), French (spoken word intro)

vocal_effects.title: Vocal Effects
vocal_effects.description: Processing and effects on the voice
vocal_effects.x-instruction: Detail any obvious studio effects applied to the lead vocals.
vocal_effects.x-example: heavy autotune, dry with no reverb, vocoder harmonies, telephone EQ filter on verse, heavy slapback delay

backing_vocals.title: Backing Vocals
backing_vocals.description: Backing vocal style, placement, role, and where they appear
backing_vocals.x-instruction: Identify any secondary vocals supporting the lead. Describe what they are doing (harmonies, ad-libs) and where they occur.
backing_vocals.x-example: lush choir harmonies on chorus only, call-and-response ad-libs throughout, gang vocal shouts on hook, layered self-harmonies stacked 6 deep, none
